wizard_meta.title: EU AI Act — System Classification Wizard
wizard_meta.description: A structured decision-tree wizard that classifies an AI system under the EU AI Act and returns the set of applicable articles. Complete all gates in order; later gates may be skipped if an earlier gate produces a definitive classification.
wizard_meta.instructions: Answer every question in each gate sequentially. Gate 1 (Prohibited) must always be completed first — a 'prohibited' result ends the assessment immediately. Gates 2–5 determine the risk tier. Gates 6–8 add cross-cutting obligations that stack on top of any risk-tier articles.
wizard_meta.output_format: applicable_articles[]  — each entry carries article_number, title, trigger_reason, and obligation_type
wizard_meta.legal_basis: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council (EU AI Act)

gates.gate_purpose: Article 5 — Identifies uses that are outright banned regardless of any other characteristic. Must be answered first.
gates.short_circuit: If ANY answer in this gate is YES → classification = PROHIBITED. Assessment ends. No other articles apply.

questions.question: Does the system use subliminal, manipulative, or deceptive techniques to distort a person's behaviour in a way that causes or is likely to cause harm?
Does the system exploit vulnerabilities of specific groups (age, disability, social/economic situation) to distort behaviour in a harmful way?
Does the system perform social scoring of natural persons by public authorities leading to detrimental or disproportionate treatment?
Does the system assess or predict the risk of a person committing a criminal offence based solely on profiling or personality traits (not objective, verifiable facts)?
Does the system compile facial recognition databases by untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage?
Does the system infer emotions of natural persons in the workplace or educational institutions (except for safety or medical reasons)?
Does the system perform biometric categorisation of natural persons to deduce or infer race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious/philosophical beliefs, or sexual orientation?
Does the system use real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes (outside the narrow Article 5(2) exemptions)?

applicable_articles.title: Prohibited AI Practices
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: One or more characteristics of the system match a practice prohibited under Article 5(1). The system may not be placed on the market, put into service, or used.

gates.gate_purpose: Article 3(1) — Confirms the system falls within the Act's definition before applying any obligations.

questions.question: Is the system a machine-based system designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy, that infers from inputs how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions?
questions.note: Traditional rule-based software with fully deterministic, handcrafted logic is NOT an AI system under the Act.

questions.question: Are the outputs produced by the system intended to influence real or virtual environments?

any_no.note: The system does not meet the Article 3(1) definition of an AI system. The EU AI Act does not apply.

gates.gate_purpose: Articles 51–56 — Identifies whether the system is or is built on a GPAI model, which carries separate obligations on top of any use-case risk tier.

questions.question: Is the system itself a foundation / large language / multimodal model trained on broad data that can perform a wide range of tasks (e.g. GPT-style, Llama, Mistral, Gemini)?
Is the system built on top of, or integrating, a third-party GPAI model via API or fine-tuning?
Was the GPAI model trained using compute exceeding 10^25 FLOPs (i.e. is it a systemic-risk model under Article 51)?
questions.note: If unknown, answer No — this threshold applies mainly to the largest frontier models.

applicable_articles.title: Obligations for Providers of GPAI Models
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The system is or incorporates a GPAI model. The GPAI model provider must maintain technical documentation, provide information to downstream deployers, and implement a copyright policy.

G3_Q1_yes_or_G3_Q2_yes_and_G3_Q3_no.note: These articles apply to the GPAI model provider. As a deployer you inherit downstream transparency obligations.

applicable_articles.title: Obligations for Providers of GPAI Models
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The system is or incorporates a GPAI model. The GPAI model provider must maintain technical documentation, provide information to downstream deployers, and implement a copyright policy.

applicable_articles.title: Obligations for Providers of GPAI Models with Systemic Risk
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The GPAI model exceeds the 10^25 FLOP systemic-risk threshold. Additional obligations apply including adversarial testing, incident reporting to the Commission, and cybersecurity measures.

gates.gate_name: High-Risk Classification — Annex I (Safety-Component of Regulated Products)
gates.gate_purpose: Annex I + Article 6(1) — Checks whether the AI system is a safety component in a product already covered by EU product-safety legislation.

questions.question: Is the AI system a safety component of, or itself a product covered by, EU legislation listed in Annex I of the AI Act (e.g. machinery, medical devices, civil aviation, automotive, marine equipment, railway, lifts, pressure equipment, personal protective equipment, gas appliances, radio equipment, in-vitro diagnostics)?
Is that product required to undergo a third-party conformity assessment under the applicable Annex I legislation?

applicable_articles.title: High-Risk Classification — Safety Component of Regulated Product
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The AI system is a safety component of an Annex I regulated product requiring third-party conformity assessment. Full Chapter III Section 2 obligations apply (Articles 8–15, 17, 72).

G4_Q1_yes_and_G4_Q2_yes.note: HIGH-RISK confirmed via Annex I path. Skip G5 and proceed to G6 for cross-cutting obligations.

gates.gate_name: High-Risk Classification — Annex III (Standalone High-Risk Use Cases)
gates.gate_purpose: Annex III + Article 6(2) — Identifies whether the use case falls within one of the eight high-risk domains listed in Annex III.
gates.instructions: Select ALL that apply. Any single YES makes the system high-risk.

questions.question: Does the system perform biometric identification or categorisation of natural persons (excluding systems for purely administrative purposes where the output does not affect legal rights)?
Is the system used in the management or operation of critical infrastructure (road traffic, water, gas, heating, electricity, rail, air)?
Is the system used in education or vocational training — e.g. determining access, admission, evaluating learning outcomes, monitoring students?
Is the system used in employment, workers management, or self-employment — e.g. recruitment, CV screening, promotion decisions, task allocation, performance monitoring?

Is the system used to evaluate eligibility for, or to assess, essential private or public services — e.g. credit scoring, insurance risk, social benefits, emergency services, public transport, utilities?
Is the system used by law enforcement authorities — e.g. individual risk assessment, polygraphs, crime analytics, evidence reliability evaluation, crime prediction, profiling?
Is the system used for migration, asylum, or border control management — e.g. risk assessment of persons, document authenticity, application processing?
Is the system used in the administration of justice or democratic processes — e.g. assisting courts in researching/interpreting facts, influencing electoral outcomes?

applicable_articles.title: High-Risk Classification — Standalone Use Case
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The system's use case matches one or more high-risk categories in Annex III. Full Chapter III Section 2 obligations apply.

applicable_articles.title: Risk Management System
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems must establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system throughout the entire lifecycle.

applicable_articles.title: Data and Data Governance
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Training, validation, and testing data for high-risk systems must meet data governance practices covering relevance, representativeness, and freedom from errors and biases.

applicable_articles.title: Technical Documentation
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers of high-risk AI systems must draw up technical documentation before the system is placed on the market, in accordance with Annex IV.

applicable_articles.title: Record-Keeping / Logging
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems must have automatic logging capabilities to enable monitoring throughout the system's lifetime.

applicable_articles.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems must provide deployers with clear instructions for use, covering intended purpose, accuracy levels, relevant risks, and human oversight measures.

applicable_articles.title: Human Oversight
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems must be designed so that natural persons can effectively oversee them during the period of use, including the ability to intervene or halt the system.

applicable_articles.title: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and be resilient against attempts to alter their use or performance by malicious third parties.

applicable_articles.title: Quality Management System
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers of high-risk AI systems must put a quality management system in place covering policies, procedures, techniques, and systematic actions needed to achieve conformity.

applicable_articles.title: Conformity Assessment
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Before placing a high-risk AI system on the market, providers must carry out a conformity assessment (self-assessment or third-party, depending on Annex I product type and Annex III point).

applicable_articles.title: Post-Market Monitoring
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers of high-risk AI systems must establish and maintain a post-market monitoring system to proactively collect and review data on performance throughout the system's lifetime.

all_no.note: System is not high-risk via Annex III. Proceed to G6 to check limited-risk transparency obligations, which may still apply.

gates.gate_purpose: Article 50 — Applies regardless of risk tier. Catches chatbots, synthetic content generators, and deep-fake tools that must disclose their AI nature to users.
gates.note: These obligations stack on top of high-risk obligations if both apply.

questions.question: Does the system interact directly with natural persons in a conversational or text-based manner (e.g. chatbot, virtual assistant, voice bot)?
Does the system generate synthetic audio, image, video, or text content that could falsely appear to be authentic (e.g. deep fakes, AI-generated media)?
Does the system generate or manipulate text that is published and could constitute information of public interest (e.g. news, policy, legal content) without clearly labelling it as AI-generated?

applicable_articles.title: Transparency Obligations for Certain AI Systems
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: The system either interacts with humans directly, generates synthetic/deceptive content, or produces AI-generated text intended for public dissemination. Users must be informed they are interacting with an AI, or that content is AI-generated, in a clear and timely manner.

gates.gate_purpose: Article 26 — Identifies whether the organisation is acting as a deployer of a high-risk AI system and therefore carries its own set of obligations beyond those of the provider.

questions.question: Is your organisation deploying a high-risk AI system that was developed by a third-party provider (i.e. you are not the original developer)?
Do natural persons in your organisation use the AI system's outputs to make decisions that affect other natural persons (employees, customers, citizens)?
Does your organisation make significant changes to the intended purpose of a high-risk AI system supplied by a third-party provider?

applicable_articles.title: Obligations of Deployers of High-Risk AI Systems
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Your organisation is a deployer of a high-risk AI system. You must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, ensure human oversight, monitor system operation, and log activity where feasible.

applicable_articles.title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Deployers of high-risk AI systems in certain public or regulated contexts must carry out a fundamental rights impact assessment before deployment.

applicable_articles.title: Responsibilities along the AI Value Chain — Substantial Modification
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Substantially modifying a high-risk AI system outside the provider's intended purpose makes the modifier responsible as a provider, triggering full Articles 8–17 obligations.

gates.gate_name: Cross-Cutting Obligations — Registration, Incidents, and Market Surveillance
gates.gate_purpose: Articles 49, 73 — Finalises the article set with registration and incident-reporting obligations that apply to all high-risk systems placed on the EU market.

questions.question: Is the high-risk AI system intended to be placed on the EU market or put into service in the EU?
Is the system already deployed or in production, meaning it is actively processing real user data?
Has the system experienced or could it experience a serious incident (malfunction, accident, or near-miss with potential harm to health, safety, or fundamental rights)?

applicable_articles.title: Registration in the EU Database
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III must register the system in the EU database managed by the Commission before placing it on the market.

applicable_articles.title: EU Declaration of Conformity
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers must draw up an EU declaration of conformity confirming the system meets all applicable high-risk requirements before market placement.

applicable_articles.title: CE Marking of Conformity
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: High-risk AI systems that are placed on the EU market must bear the CE marking indicating conformity with the Act's requirements.

applicable_articles.title: Reporting of Serious Incidents
applicable_articles.trigger_reason: Providers and deployers must report serious incidents or malfunctions that constitute a breach of obligations under EU law protecting fundamental rights to national market surveillance authorities without undue delay.

article_reference_map.description: Master reference of all AI Act articles that can be returned by this wizard, for linking into your compliance framework JSON.

articles.title: High-Risk Classification — Annex I Safety Component
High-Risk Classification — Annex III Standalone Use Case
Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
Obligations of Deployers of High-Risk AI Systems
Transparency Obligations for Certain AI Systems
Obligations for Providers of GPAI Models
harmonised_standards_hint: EU AI Office GPAI Code of Practice

articles.title: Obligations for Providers of GPAI Models with Systemic Risk
harmonised_standards_hint: EU AI Office GPAI Code of Practice

PROHIBITED.description: System matches Article 5 banned practices. Cannot be placed on the market or put into service.

OUT_OF_SCOPE.description: System does not meet the Article 3(1) definition of an AI system. Act does not apply.

HIGH_RISK.description: Full Chapter III Section 2 obligations apply. Conformity assessment required before market placement.

LIMITED_RISK.description: No high-risk use case identified. Only Article 50 transparency obligations apply if chatbot/synthetic content features are present.

MINIMAL_RISK.description: No prohibited practice, not high-risk, no chatbot or synthetic-content features. No mandatory AI Act obligations, though voluntary codes of practice are encouraged.
